content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Jan Weiss 
Walnut Grove Fire District 
P.O. Box 41 
Walnut Grove, CA 95690 
Dear Mr. Weiss: 
June 3, 1987 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. I-87-133 
You have written seeking advice on behalf of Jerry 
Himebauch, Assistant Chief of the Walnut Grove Fire District. 
Your letter seeks advice regarding past conduct by Mr. 
Himebauch. 
The Commission does not provide advice relating to 
past conduct. 
(Commission Regulation 18329(b) (8) (A); copy 
enclosed. ) 
Your letter also may raise issues involving interpretation 
of Government Code section 1090. 
That statute is not part of 
the Political Reform Act. 
Consequently, we cannot provide 
advice as to its applicability. 
You might discuss this matter 
with your local city attorney. 
If you have any questions concerning this letter, please 
feel free to give me a call at (916) 322-5901. 
DMG:jaj 
Enclosure 
xncerely, 
UCvr-.-. ·fn-::J.-ft~j~ 
Diane M. 
Griffith~ . 
General Counsel 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660 


P. O. BOX 41 
April 29, 1987 
Diane Griffiths 
Lf28 "J" St. 
800 
Sacramento, CA 95814 
Dear Diane: 
RE: 
Conflict of Interest 
Exemption 
The Walnut Grove Fire District has just been 
audited and a Confli.ct of Interest question 
came up once again. 
The situation is this: 
Our Assistant Chief, 
Jerry Himebauch, owns and operates Himebauch's 
Auto Parts and Service here in Walnut Grove. 
Jerry is not on the Board of Directors. 
All 
or most 
0 
the work on our emergency vehicles 
is done by Himebauch's. 
Our Board of Directors 
and Chairman, Joe Enos are concerned about a 
possible Confljct of Interest violation and 
would like a ruling of exemption for our files. 
Per our conversation, I am writing for such a 
ruling by your office. 
I 
spoke with Jerry today 
and he is aware or the situation and will help 
out any way he can. 
His address, as you requE'stl:'d: 
Jerry Him bauch 
PO Box 12 
o c:: rc, n I) 
J 
J 
\.J./ V 
I hope this will be sufficient information 
to obtain the Conflict of Interest exemption 
and/or ruling from your office. 
If I can be 
of 
urther help, please advise.

16-776-19 5 
WALNUT GROVE, CALIFORNIA 95690 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Jan Weiss, secretary 
Walnut Grove Fire District 
P.o. Box 41 
Walnut Grove, CA 
95690 
Dear Ms. Weiss: 
May 12, 1987 
Re: 
87-133 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on May 4, 1987 by the Fair Political Practices 
Commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice 
request, you may contact Kathryn E. Donovan, an attorney in the 
Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If your request is for informal assistance, we will 
answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission Regulation 
18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware 
that your letter and our response are public records which may 
be disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for 
disclosure. 
Very truly yours, 
~~i~ 
General Counsel 
DMG:jas

sections.conclusion: The Commission does not provide advice relating to past conduct. Your letter also may raise issues involving interpretation of Government Code section 1090. That statute is not part of the Political Reform Act. Consequently, we cannot provide advice as to its applicability. You might discuss this matter with your local city attorney.
sections.question_synthetic: The Walnut Grove Fire District sought FPPC advice regarding whether Assistant Chief Jerry Himebauch's ownership of an auto parts business that performed work on the Fire District's emergency vehicles constituted a conflict of interest, and requested an exemption ruling for their files.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide advice because the request concerned past conduct, which falls outside the Commission's authority under Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A). Additionally, the matter may involve Government Code section 1090, which is outside the Political Reform Act's scope, and the requestor should consult their local city attorney.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request (April 29, 1987 letter from Jan Weiss) and FPPC responses (May 12, 1987 acknowledgment letter and June 3, 1987 substantive response). The substantive advice letter (June 3, 1987) is the primary FPPC response analyzed. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections with headers, but content clearly delineates request and response.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The Walnut Grove Fire District sought FPPC advice regarding whether Assistant Chief Jerry Himebauch's ownership of an auto parts business that performed work on the Fire District's emergency vehicles constituted a conflict of interest, and requested an exemption ruling for their files.

CONCLUSION: The Commission does not provide advice relating to past conduct. Your letter also may raise issues involving interpretation of Government Code section 1090. That statute is not part of the Political Reform Act. Consequently, we cannot provide advice as to its applicability. You might discuss this matter with your local city attorney.

embedding.summary: The Walnut Grove Fire District requested FPPC advice regarding a potential conflict of interest involving an Assistant Chief who owned an auto parts business performing work for the Fire District. The FPPC declined to advise, citing its prohibition on providing guidance about past conduct and noting that the matter may implicate Government Code section 1090, which is outside the FPPC's jurisdiction.
